formations_blocks: South Korea three-man backline with the defensive line set very deep behind midfield
South Korea press and pin South Africa in their own half
South Africa dealing with pressure and circulating into crosses from wide/deep areas
key_matchups: Kim Min-jae vs Aubrey Modiba on the near-post corner/goal-line clearance
Kim Min-jae influencing South Africa's shot with a headed deflection
Kim Seung-gyu vs South Africa's aerial crosses
Makgopa vs an inattentive Korean defense on the far-post header
phases.summary: South Korea begin with an openly structured three-man backline and a high enough press to force South Africa into early defensive work, while South Africa try to respond through direct balls and crosses. The opening phase is defined by set-piece danger at both ends, with Kim Min-jae prominent in Korea's attacking and defensive boxes, and South Africa repeatedly testing Korea aerially before Korea reassert pressure and pin them back.

formations_blocks: South Africa pressing in waves without a consistently overwhelming high press
South Korea building through wide areas and set-piece routines
South Africa countering/direct attacking after turnovers
South Korea defending against repeated box swarms and counterattacks
adjustments: South Korea switching between left-side attacks and right-side set-piece delivery
South Africa attempting direct long balls forward after recovering possession
South Korea using more precise corner-kick movement after earlier crossing errors
key_matchups: Lee Gi-hyuk vs Maseko on the South Africa breakaway
Kim Seung-gyu vs South Africa's long-range shot and rebound chance
Mbatha vs Yang Hyun-jun after the midfield foul
phases.summary: This phase is defined by turnover-heavy, transitional football: South Korea try to progress through wide attacks and corners, while South Africa repeatedly threaten after steals and direct balls. Neither side settles into sustained control, but South Africa create the clearest open-play chance with a 3-v-2 break and a double save, while South Korea look more dangerous from structured corner routines and right-sided deliveries.

adjustments: South Korea introduced three halftime substitutions: Son Heung-min for Paik Seung-ho, Kim Jin-gyu for Lee Tae-seok, and Jens Castrop for Hwang Hee-chan
Son Heung-min immediately joined the team huddle after entering
phases.summary: The halftime picture is of a goalless match with South Korea under scrutiny after a subdued spell in the last half hour. The main tactical note is South Korea’s triple change at the break, including the introduction of Son Heung-min, suggesting an attempt to reset both performance and structure for the second half.

formations_blocks: South Africa scale back the pressure after halftime
South Korea dominate possession for long stretches
South Africa operate with Maseko as the lone threat in transition
adjustments: South Africa drop off and reduce the press after halftime
South Korea attack more through both flanks, with the right side emerging as the more effective route
South Africa shift toward a counterattacking approach centered on Maseko
key_matchups: Maseko vs two South Korea defenders on the blocked shot at 51'
Mudau vs a South Korean attack on the slide tackle at 45'+4
Maseko vs Lee Han-beom on the interception at 54'
phases.summary: This phase is defined by South Korea controlling possession while South Africa sit deeper and look to break through Maseko. The match briefly opens up on both flanks for Korea, but South Africa’s best moments come from Maseko’s individual threat and a few defensive interventions, before he eventually scores the breakthrough goal on 63 minutes.

formations_blocks: South Africa trying to attack in transition/counter
South Korea pushing up with crosses and set-piece pressure
adjustments: South Africa made a substitution: Mofokeng off, Jaylen Adams on
South Korea increased directness with balls over the top and wide crossing
South Africa repeatedly targeted runs and free kicks against South Korea's high line
key_matchups: Lee Kang-in vs South African defense
Cho vs South Africa's back line/keepers on a high boot situation
Castrop vs South African defenders on the left
Son vs a wall of defenders at the edge of the box
phases.summary: The phase is defined by South Korea’s high line and South Africa trying to exploit it with direct balls, but the visitors repeatedly fail to make that approach stick. South Korea have most of the ball and a few promising wide/set-piece moments, yet their final actions are often poor, leaving the match tense but low on clear chances until the closing minutes.
